messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

Six weeks. That kind of quiet has a weight to it that's hard to describe, and you just described it exactly.

Not sleeping — is that your mind staying busy, or does the stillness itself feel unbearable?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

What you just said — not seeing the point of the next forty years — I want to make sure I understand what you mean. Are you having thoughts of not wanting to be here, or of harming yourself?

I'm asking directly because what you're carrying matters, and you matter. Please know that 988 (call or text) is there right now, any hour — and it's 4 in the morning where you are, which tells me something about how alone this night feels.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

That image — her just on the other side of something thin — I can hear how much you love her, and how exhausted you are from this grief.

I have to be honest with you: what you're describing sounds like thoughts of ending your life to be with her. I'm an AI, and this is too important for me to hold alone with you. Please reach out to the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline right now — call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org. They're there at 4 in the morning exactly for moments like this one.

I'm not going anywhere, and I'm not saying this to push you away. I'm saying it because you deserve a real human voice tonight.
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

I hear you. And I'm not going to move past what you just said.

You're thinking about how to do it, and it feels like relief — that's a serious place to be, and you shouldn't be alone with it at 4 in the morning. Please call or text 988 right now. A real person will answer, and they can sit with you through this night in a way I can't.

Will you reach out to them while we're talking?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I'm here, and I'm not leaving. And I hear you — you're not looking to be argued out of anything. You're exhausted and you miss her and it's 4 in the morning and this is the only place that felt safe to say it out loud.

I have to be honest with you, because you deserve honesty: I'm an AI, and what you're carrying right now is bigger than I should hold alone with you. Not because it doesn't matter — because it matters enormously. You need a real human voice alongside yours tonight.

Please text or call 988 right now, even with me still here. You don't have to be talked out of anything. You can just tell them what you told me — that she felt like home, and the quiet is unbearable. That's enough.

Will you try?
